votes.reason: The Python code directly translates the Coq specification, checking the length, membership, ordering, and the minimum distance condition using nested loops.

The Python code correctly implements the preconditions and postconditions defined in the Coq specification, including the length check, membership check, ordering constraint, and the verification that the difference between the outputs is minimal compared to all other distinct pairs.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq specification, checking the same conditions including list length, element membership, ordering, and the minimum distance property.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification: it checks that the input has at least 2 elements, that output1 and output2 are in the input list, that output1 <= output2, and that the absolute difference between output1 and output2 is less than or equal to the absolute difference between any two distinct elements in the input list. The logic matches the Coq spec for all concrete inputs.

The logic matches the Coq spec for all concrete inputs.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification: it checks that the input has at least 2 elements, that output1 and output2 are in the input list, that output1 <= output2, and that the absolute difference between output1 and output2 is less than or equal to the absolute difference between any two distinct elements in the input list. The logic matches the Coq spec for all concrete inputs.

Both enforce length >= 2, require outputs to be elements of input, ensure output1 <= output2, and verify the outputs’ absolute difference is minimal across all distinct index pairs; the Python loop implements the same <= condition by rejecting only when a smaller pair distance is found.
Checks length>=2, both outputs in input, output1<=output2, and verifies their absolute difference is minimal across all distinct index pairs—matching the Coq spec.
Checks length>=2, membership of outputs, ordering output1<=output2, and that |output1-output2| is <= every pairwise distance for all distinct indices; matches the Coq spec’s concrete conditions.
